papers.title: Redshift-Dependent Intrinsic Dispersion in the Quasar UV/X-ray Luminosity Relation
papers.summary: Accurate modeling of the intrinsic dispersion in the quasar UV/X-ray luminosity relation is essential for reliable cosmological inference. We investigate its redshift dependence using luminosity distances reconstructed from cosmic chronometer and baryon acoustic oscillation measurements through Gaussian-process (GP) regression. Bayesian model comparison and posterior constraints show that the intrinsic dispersion is not well described by a single redshift-independent constant over $0.7<z<2.6$. It remains approximately constant at $0.7<z<1.6$, but shows an overall decreasing trend in the higher-redshift interval $1.6<z<2.6$, where the redshift-dependent intrinsic-dispersion model is decisively favored. This conclusion remains qualitatively robust against changes in the scaling-relation parameterization, GP kernel, and redshift binning scheme. We further examine its impact on cosmological inference in the flat $Λ$CDM model and find that, under the adopted calibration setup, the redshift-dependent intrinsic-dispersion model shifts the posterior median of $Ω_{\rm m0}$ by $ΔΩ_{\rm m0}\simeq 0.025$. This indicates that intrinsic-dispersion modeling is a non-negligible component of the systematic-error budget for quasar cosmology and should be accounted for in future precision analyses.

papers.title: Clustering of high-redshift quasars with DESI DR2
papers.summary: We present clustering measurements for high-redshift quasars using data from the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument Data Release 2. Our sample consists of quasars with $2.0 < z < 3.5$ in the luminosity range $M_{1450} \leq -19.94$\,mag. We measure the mean quasar bias $b_Q(\bar{z} = 2.48) = 3.61 \pm 0.01$ for the full sample of $\sim 715,000$ quasars and quantify the redshift evolution of quasar bias by dividing the sample into four equal redshift bins. There is strong evolution of the quasar bias with redshift that is well fit by the function $b_Q(z) = a [(1 + z)^2 - 6.565] + b$ with $a=0.230 \pm 0.007$ and $b=2.394 \pm 0.035$, and this fit is also a good match to lower redshift measurements in the literature. This bias evolution is consistent with a characteristic halo mass of $\bar{M}_{\mathrm{h}} \sim 10^{12}\,\mathrm{M_\odot}$ that does not vary significantly with redshift. The inferred duty cycles for quasars in our sample are $f_{\mathrm{duty}} \sim 10^{-2}$, staying mostly constant over redshifts. We investigate the luminosity dependence of quasar clustering by dividing each of our four redshift bins into three luminosity bins. The size of our quasar sample permits the first statistically significant measurement of the luminosity dependence of quasar bias at these redshifts. We measure weak dependence of quasar bias on luminosity at fixed redshift, inconsistent with no dependence, but weaker than predicted by a model in which quasar luminosity is tightly correlated with halo mass. These clustering measurements provide a stringent test for models of active black hole light curves and the black hole-halo connection at high redshift.

papers.title: Quintom Model Perturbations
papers.summary: We build upon the work of Goh and Taylor 2025, in which we proposed a two scalar field quintom framework capable of naturally realising a phantom-to-quintessence transition in the dark energy equation of state. In this work, we derive the linear perturbation equations of our model and investigate its implications for large scale structure formation. We show that the quintom model is able to reproduce the phenomenological features of a w0waCDM cosmology with phantom-to-quintessence crossing, including suppressions in the matter power spectrum and enhancements to the late-time Integrated Sachs-Wolfe effect. We then perform a Bayesian analysis of the quintom framework with BAO, CMB and Type 1a supernovae data, finding that it is mildly favoured over the standard w0waCDM parametrisation, while successfully reproducing physical trends observed in the data. We further examine the parameter degeneracies inherent to the model and discuss prospective observational strategies for distinguishing quintom cosmologies from conventional dynamical dark energy models given current and future data precision.

papers.title: Probing inflationary particle production with the CMB power spectrum
papers.summary: Particle production is common to many microphysical models of inflation and can imprint observable features in the cosmic microwave background (CMB) anisotropies. We consider a scenario in which the inflaton couples to an extremely massive field ($m_χ\gtrsim \mathcal{O}(100 H_I)$, where $H_I$ is the inflationary Hubble scale). In this model, particle production happens in a burst at a characteristic conformal time, $η_*$, which sources localized features in the CMB. In this paper, we compute the full temperature and polarization two-point functions for this model. We then search for these features in CMB power spectrum data from Planck and the Atacama Cosmology Telescope (ACT), with the latter allowing access to features on smaller angular scales. In the joint analysis of Planck and ACT data, we find a mild $\sim 2 σ$ hint for a signal induced by this inflationary model on scales $3 \,\, \text{Mpc}\leqη_*\leq 10 \,\, \text{Mpc}$, though this hint is not present at a statistically significant level in either dataset when analyzed individually. Using a Fisher forecast, we find that these features should be observable at the $3-5σ$ level for a Simons Observatory-like experiment, if they are indeed real. We also compare our power-spectrum-based constraints to previous matched-filter-based bounds on this model. For sufficiently light particles ($m_χ\lesssim 200 H_I$), the power spectrum yields tighter constraints by more than an order of magnitude, but in the higher-mass regime where particle production is rare, the matched-filter approach provides stronger bounds.

papers.title: SKA VLBI survey of the Southern sky for astrometry, geodesy, and astrophysics
papers.summary: The development of SKA will open the opportunity to run dedicated VLBI surveys of the Southern sky and observe sources not visible at radio telescopes located in the Northern hemisphere. These surveys will allow for doing geodesy with a Southern hemisphere-centered network, increase the density of compact radio sources that can be used as calibrators, further extend the celestial reference frame to deep south, and facilitate high-precision differential astrometry for a wide range of applications. Achieving a deep completeness level and determining the parsec-scale properties of extragalactic radio sources is crucial for multi-wavelength and multi-messenger astrophysics. This includes supporting Cherenkov and neutrino telescope science cases, as well as joint VLBI-Gaia studies of active galaxies.

papers.title: Columba: isolated dwarf galaxy populations in diverse cosmological environments simulated with a cold interstellar medium
papers.summary: We introduce a suite of LambdaCDM cosmological, hydrodynamical simulations that track the evolution of a large population of dwarf galaxies. The suite comprises zoom-in simulations of 25 spherical, under-dense regions of r=5cMpc, selected to span $\approx1.5$ dex in mean enclosed density, covering voids to filamentary structures, whilst excluding haloes of Milky Way-mass or larger. The simulations achieve a mass resolution of $\sim 10^5$ solar masses with a galaxy formation model including cold, dense interstellar gas and whose subgrid stellar feedback efficiency reproduces the z=0 galaxy stellar mass function. We investigate the impact of the cosmic environment on dwarf galaxy formation and evolution. We find that the 5 cMpc environment influences the normalisation of the halo and galaxy mass functions, but does not significantly affect the stellar mass - halo mass (SMHM) relation and halo occupation fraction for galaxies with $M_{\star}=10^6-10^9$ solar masses. Instead, host halo concentration, estimated from DM-only counterparts, is more important: both the fraction of haloes hosting a resolved galaxy and the scatter about the SMHM relation correlate positively with concentration. Owing to halo assembly bias, concentration also influences galaxy formation times, such that at fixed halo mass more concentrated haloes host galaxies that are both older and more massive. The offset from the mean SMHM relation also anti-correlates with $t_{90}$, the time at which 90 percent of a galaxy's stellar mass has assembled. These correlations between halo properties and galaxy star formation histories present testable predictions for forthcoming observational surveys.

papers.title: Initial Mass Functions of Young Stellar Clusters from the Gemini Spectroscopic Survey of Nearby Galaxies. II. Young Clusters in NGC 1313
papers.summary: We present a spectroscopic study of young stellar clusters in the barred spiral galaxy NGC 1313. Integrated light spectra of 11 clusters, obtained using the GMOS-S instrument on the 8.1 m Gemini South telescope, are analyzed using a simple stellar population model. A subsolar metallicity (Z = 0.008) is adopted, consistent with previous studies. Cluster ages are constrained primarily through absorption lines and prominent emission bands of Wolf-Rayet stars. Utilizing these constraints, we match the observed spectra with synthetic counterparts generated from the simple stellar population model, determining key physical parameters including age, cluster mass, and the underlying initial mass function (IMF). Furthermore, the impact of stochastic sampling on the derived parameters of several low-mass clusters is rigorously evaluated using Monte Carlo simulations. The sampled clusters exhibit ages ranging from 2.5 to 300 Myr and stellar masses between 2.8 x 10^3 Msun and 2.6 x 10^5 Msun. Notably, for stellar masses exceeding 0.8 Msun, the power-law index (Gamma) of the underlying IMFs is found to be smaller than the standard Salpeter/Kroupa IMF. Furthermore, a correlation is observed where more massive clusters tend to possess top-light IMFs. This finding aligns with trends observed in the young clusters of the Antennae Galaxies, despite the differing mass scales between the two systems. Our results suggest that applying a universal standard IMF to spatially unresolved systems warrants caution, given the inherent complexities revealed in this study.

papers.title: Deep Imaging of Grus II and Horologium II: Structure and Extent of Two Ultra-Faint Milky Way Satellites
papers.summary: We present deep, wide-field Magellan/Megacam imaging of the ultra-faint Milky Way (MW) satellites Grus II (Gru II) and Horologium II (Hor II), with the aim of deriving improved constraints on their distances, luminosities, and structural parameters, while also searching for possible signs of tidal disturbance. Our photometry reaches approximately 3 magnitudes deeper than the discovery data, enabling robust measurements of these quantities. Both systems exhibit color-magnitude diagrams consistent with old ($\sim$12.5 Gyr), very metal-poor stellar populations. We find Gru II to be at a distance of $52.3 \pm 1.9$ kpc, with a half-light radius of $6.8 \pm 0.5$ arcmin (103 $\pm$ 9 pc), ellipticity $ε= 0.25 \pm 0.07$, and absolute magnitude $M_V = -4.07 \pm 0.50$ mag. Hor II is further away at a distance of $72.4^{+5.9}_{-5.5}$ kpc and more compact, with $r_h = 2.1 \pm 0.2$ arcmin (44$^{+6}_{-5}$ pc), $ε= 0.32^{+0.20}_{-0.16}$, and $M_V = -2.10 \pm 0.44$ mag. Both galaxies lie within the typical size-luminosity locus of MW ultra-faint dwarfs. Gru II shows an asymmetric morphology including multi-directional clumpy features, some of which may be suggestive of tidal disturbance. We further identify and spectroscopically confirm a new distant member just outside $3r_h$ in Gru II, providing independent evidence for member stars at large projected radii. In contrast, Hor II appears regular, with no significant extended structure detected to the surface-brightness limits of our data.

papers.title: Exploring Gravitational Wave Signatures Due to Primordial Non-gaussianity and Large Scale Structure Using SKAO
papers.summary: This chapter explores theoretical and observational strategies to use the stochastic gravitational-wave background detectable by Square Kilometre Array Observatory (SKAO) as a probe of precision cosmology. We detail the critical phenomenon of scalar-induced gravitational waves, demonstrating their unique features and their sensitivity to primordial non-Gaussianity on scales much smaller than those probed by the cosmic microwave background and large-scale structure. We investigate the phenomenology of parity violation in the early Universe through the chirality imprinted in the stochastic gravitational-wave background, demonstrating that a parity-odd primordial trispectrum can generate a detectable scale-dependent helicity. On the observational side, we point out that the standard gravitational-wave angular auto-correlation analysis is significantly limited by astrophysical shot noise. We show that cross-correlating the gravitational wave signal with independent large-scale structure tracers enhances the signal-to-noise ratio and allows us to isolate the astrophysical and cosmological components in the background. These results can be achieved only thanks to the enhanced sensitivity of SKAO, extensive sky coverage, and high angular resolution, which together make such targets observationally feasible.

papers.title: Using SKAO to Understand the Clustering of Gravitational Wave Sources
papers.summary: Coalescing Binary Black Holes (BBHs) trace the Large-Scale Structure (LSS) of the Universe, and their clustering properties can be extracted from Gravitational Wave (GW) data. Next-generation detectors, such as the Einstein Telescope and Cosmic Explorer, will enable statistical studies of GW sources thanks to the massive number of detected events. However, such events will still suffer from significant instrumental and theoretical uncertainties. Cross-correlating GW maps with other LSS surveys provides a promising strategy to mitigate these limitations. The SKA-Mid intensity mapping and radio continuum surveys offer ideal datasets for cross-correlation studies with GWs (SKAO$\times$ET2CE). Their wide sky coverage and deep redshift sensitivity will allow precise probing of the epochs and environments where stellar BBHs form most efficiently. In this chapter, we forecast the potential of cross-correlation angular power spectra to extract information on the distribution and clustering properties of GW events. First, we model the number density and bias of three independent tracers: GW sources, neutral hydrogen intensity maps, and radio galaxies. We estimate the constraining power of SKA-Mid$\times$ET2CE on the GW clustering bias, which carries information on the origin of GW progenitors, e.g., whether they formed through stellar evolution or are primordial black holes. Finally, we develop a semi-analytic model for GW events hosted by SKAO galaxies as a function of the time-delay distribution between the binary formation and merger, which is still largely uncertain to date. We forecast the signal-to-noise ratio of their cross-correlation with SKA-Mid, and demonstrate that SKA-Mid$\times$ET2CE will foster our understanding of the time-delay distribution.

papers.title: Measuring local primordial non-Gaussianity from the clustering of DESI DR1 LRGs and QSOs
papers.summary: We report the first measurement of primordial non-Gaussianity (PNG), parameterized by $f_{\mathrm{NL}}$, in the configuration space two-point correlation function (2pcf). We employ simulation based modeling and a novel approach for the mitigation of imaging systematics. We apply this method to samples of luminous red galaxies (LRG) and quasars (QSO) observed by the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) during the first year of its observations (DR1). The observed 68\% CL interval on $f_{\mathrm{NL}}$ is $-3^{+22}_{-21}$ using LRGs, and $ 0^{+17}_{-16}$ using QSOs. The joint measurement yields $f_{\mathrm{NL}} = -3^{+12}_{-12}$ at $\ [68\%]$ CL. Our pipeline imposes a Gaussian prior on the value of $p$ (which defines the PNG bias via the Universality relation), with $p_{\rm LRG} = 1.0 \pm 0.1$ and $p_{\rm QSO} = 1.6\pm 0.1$. The observed constraining power of DESI tracers significantly exceeds that of previous large-scale structure (LSS) surveys, and encouragingly, approaches the sensitivity of CMB probes of PNG.

papers.title: Cosmological constraints from the DESI DR1 Bispectrum Full-Shape and DR2 BAO
papers.summary: We present cosmological constraints from the combination of DESI DR1 full-shape measurements, including for the LRG bispectrum, and DESI DR2 BAO data. The joint analysis accounts for cross-covariance using mocks, while ShapeFit compression mitigates prior volume effects that hinder beyond-$Λ$CDM analyses. In $Λ$CDM, the bispectrum (P+B) shifts $σ_8$ up by $1.1σ$ and $S_8$ by $1.2σ$, reducing their uncertainties by $26\%$ and $28\%$, respectively. For $w_0w_a$CDM, DESI-only analyses with the bispectrum shift dark energy parameters toward $Λ$CDM, staying consistent with a cosmological constant within $1σ$. Adding CMB creates a preference for evolving dark energy: DESI+CMB (P+B) shows a $2.8σ$ deviation from $Λ$CDM. Including DES-Dovekie supernovae alone reduces this to $1.6σ$, while the full combination DESI+CMB+DES-Dovekie gives $3.1σ$, driven primarily by the CMB. The bispectrum consistently weakens evidence for time-varying dark energy relative to power-spectrum-only analyses. The bispectrum also enhances sensitivity to massive neutrinos: in DESI-only analysis, the power-spectrum-only posterior for $\sum m_ν$ is consistent with zero, whereas adding the bispectrum yields a mean of $0.26\pm0.17$~eV and a $95\%$ upper limit of $0.57$~eV, shifting the peak into the positive region and agreeing with oscillation lower bounds. For modified gravity, the bispectrum further constrains $μ_0 = 0.12\pm0.49$ from DESI-only data, consistent with general relativity. Our analysis shows that accounting for cross-dataset covariances and avoiding prior volume effects yields robust constraints, with the bispectrum raising amplitude parameters and tightening their uncertainties.

papers.title: Red vs. Blue: How metallicity shapes black hole dynamics and mergers in dense star clusters
papers.summary: Dense star clusters are a well-established environment for the formation of gravitational wave sources through dynamical interactions. Recent LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA (LVK) events such as GW241011 and GW241110 provide some of the best evidence yet for a dynamical origin. However, their relatively low component masses are in tension with predictions from low-metallicity globular cluster models (which typically produce more massive black holes), hinting that these events may have originated in higher-metallicity environments. Here we present a new set of Monte Carlo star cluster simulations with refined coverage in metallicity, focusing specifically on clusters with [Fe/H] $\geq-1$, similar to the ''red'' globular cluster subpopulation observed in most galaxies. We show that metallicity has a significant effect on the mass function of black holes and black hole mergers, the total number of black hole mergers per cluster, black hole retention from natal kicks, the mass segregation time for black-hole-driven cluster dynamics, and the merger delay time distribution. We also show that high-metallicity cluster models produce low-mass hierarchical mergers consistent with the mass ratios and component masses of GW241011 and GW241110, motivating the importance of high-metallicity clusters in the astrophysical interpretation of future LVK catalogs.

papers.title: Red vs. Blue: How metallicity shapes black hole dynamics and mergers in dense star clusters
papers.summary: Dense star clusters are a well-established environment for the formation of gravitational wave sources through dynamical interactions. papers.title: A cross-epoch endpoint-consistency test of a single effective scaling from dark energy to inflation
papers.summary: A cross-epoch endpoint-consistency test is formulated for a single power-law effective scaling, $M_U(μ)=β^{1/4}\,M_P(μ/M_P)^γ$, connecting late-time cosmic acceleration to the inflationary energy scale. The normalization is anchored at $μ=H_0$ by the late-time dark energy closure relations of the density-responsive gravity (DRG) framework and extrapolated to the inflationary Hubble rate $μ=H_*$. Comparison with the CMB-normalized Starobinsky plateau defines the residual matching factor $C_{\rm infl}\equiv V_0^{A_s}/V_0^{\rm RG}$. The novelty is the formulation of the inflation - dark-energy connection as a quantitative endpoint test between two empirically anchored energy scales. The lever arm $H_*/H_0\sim 10^{55}$ compresses endpoint uncertainties into $δγ\propto [p\ln(H_*/H_0)]^{-1}δ\ln X$, so requiring $C_{\rm infl}=\mathcal{O}(1)$ selects a narrow consistency band. For the benchmark $V_0\propto M_U^4$ with $c_4=\mathcal{O}(1)$, matching requires $γ\simeq 0.491$ and $β\simeq 0.68$, both $\mathcal{O}(1)$. Varying $p\in[2,8]$ shifts $γ$ only mildly (approximately $0.45$--$0.52$), with natural matching favoring $p\simeq 3$--$4$. We present the full uncertainty budget, including cumulative threshold effects ($Ξ$) and observational errors on the late-time anchor ($w_0$, $A_s$, $H_*$). A secondary consequence is that the effective vacuum sector dilutes as $ρ_Φ\propto a^{-6γ}$ at late times, faster than spatial curvature for $γ>1/3$. In a closed universe, this permits a cosmological turnaround at $|Ω_K|\sim\mathcal{O}(10^{-4})$, testable with Stage-IV surveys and qualitatively distinct from $Λ$CDM.

papers.title: A Potential Signature of HD 7977's Passage Among Observed Long-Period Comet Orbits
papers.summary: It is generally presumed that the tidal field of the Milky Way's disk is the main perturbation that has driven observed long-period comets (LPCs) from the Oort cloud into the inner solar system. The tide's influence on the Oort cloud should produce a distinct anisotropy in the arguments of perihelion ($ω$) of dynamically new LPCs with semimajor axes ($a$) over 10$^4$ au. Simulating LPC production dominated by the Galactic tide, we find that observed dynamically new LPCs are more isotropic than expected. Meanwhile, our simulation exhibits much better agreement between simulated and observed ``returning'' LPCs that have made a handful of passages through the inner solar system prior to discovery. The isotropy of new LPCs can be explained if the Oort cloud is much less centrally concentrated than the conventional Oort cloud formation model predicts. However, a second possibility also exists. Additional simulations we perform show that the observed $ω$ distributions of new and returning LPCs can both be well-replicated if the star HD 7977 passed within $\sim$6000--10000 au of the Sun $\sim$2.5 Myrs ago. In such a scenario, our solar system is still undergoing the latter stages of a comet shower. These simulations imply the modern observed LPC flux is $\sim$twice as high as the longer-term (tide-dominated) rate. This also implies that estimates of the Oort cloud's population should be revised downward by a factor of $\sim$2. Our LPC analysis predicts the upcoming Gaia data release will favor an HD 7977 impact parameter of $\sim$6000--10000 au.

papers.title: Deep Imaging of Grus II and Horologium II: Structure and Extent of Two Ultra-Faint Milky Way Satellites
papers.summary: We present deep, wide-field Magellan/Megacam imaging of the ultra-faint Milky Way (MW) satellites Grus II (Gru II) and Horologium II (Hor II), with the aim of deriving improved constraints on their distances, luminosities, and structural parameters, while also searching for possible signs of tidal disturbance. Our photometry reaches approximately 3 magnitudes deeper than the discovery data, enabling robust measurements of these quantities. Both systems exhibit color-magnitude diagrams consistent with old ($\sim$12.5 Gyr), very metal-poor stellar populations. We find Gru II to be at a distance of $52.3 \pm 1.9$ kpc, with a half-light radius of $6.8 \pm 0.5$ arcmin (103 $\pm$ 9 pc), ellipticity $ε= 0.25 \pm 0.07$, and absolute magnitude $M_V = -4.07 \pm 0.50$ mag. Hor II is further away at a distance of $72.4^{+5.9}_{-5.5}$ kpc and more compact, with $r_h = 2.1 \pm 0.2$ arcmin (44$^{+6}_{-5}$ pc), $ε= 0.32^{+0.20}_{-0.16}$, and $M_V = -2.10 \pm 0.44$ mag. Both galaxies lie within the typical size-luminosity locus of MW ultra-faint dwarfs. Gru II shows an asymmetric morphology including multi-directional clumpy features, some of which may be suggestive of tidal disturbance. We further identify and spectroscopically confirm a new distant member just outside $3r_h$ in Gru II, providing independent evidence for member stars at large projected radii. In contrast, Hor II appears regular, with no significant extended structure detected to the surface-brightness limits of our data.

papers.title: The 3D clustering of Lyman Alpha Emitters measured with DESI
papers.summary: We present a clustering analysis of Lyman-$α$ emitters (LAEs) using spectroscopic observations from the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) of candidates selected from the Blanco/DECam Intermediate-Band Imaging Survey (IBIS). We measure the two-point correlation function and the power spectrum, including cross-correlations with DESI quasars. Using both analytical and halo occupation distribution (HOD) simulation-based modeling, we find a linear bias of $b \sim 2.31$--$2.62$ for LAEs over the redshift range $2.26 < z < 3.41$. The analytical modeling also provides constraints on the strength of radiative transfer effects, while the HOD analysis characterizes the LAE-halo connection across multiple models. Finally, we quantify the magnitude of non-perturbative clustering effects such as Fingers of God in the LAE population, providing essential input for the accurate modeling of LAE-based cosmological analyses in forthcoming high-redshift surveys such as DESI-II.

papers.summary: Coalescing Binary Black Holes (BBHs) trace the Large-Scale Structure (LSS) of the Universe, and their clustering properties can be extracted from Gravitational Wave (GW) data. papers.title: Evaluating the Sensitivity of the Age Inferences of Red Giant Stars to Machine Learning Methodology
papers.summary: Stellar ages are vital for understanding the formation of our galaxy, but they are among the most challenging parameters to measure. Many authors address this by using machine learning models trained on stars of known age. Here we used data for 351,995 stars from Milky Way Mapper Data Release 19 to explore the sensitivity of the inferred ages to 1) neural network hyperparameters, 2) machine learning architecture, and 3) training set. We find that the resulting ages are generally insensitive to the neural network hyperparameters or the machine learning architecture, but are somewhat sensitive to the training set chosen. We also find that ages for the oldest, coolest, and lowest metallicity stars in the sample are most sensitive to the methodology used and the training set chosen. In general, our analysis suggests that even simple neural network models are sufficient for accurate age inference, but future work expanding the available training sets will be an important component of predicting reliable ages for the full galactic population.

papers.title: Dual-disk galaxies and thermal states of circumgalactic medium
papers.summary: Disk galaxies often have dual-disk structures composed of thin disks and thick disks. Recent observations reveal that such duality is ubiquitous across broad galaxy mass and cosmic time. It is suggested that more massive galaxies have smaller mass fractions of thick disks and undergo a transition from thick (or single) disk states to dual-disk states at earlier times. Previous studies suggest that the circumgalactic medium (CGM) in halos above the critical mass is heated by the development of stable shocks and feedback from active galactic nuclei. It has been argued that this thermal change of the CGM causes the transition of the accretion of CGM from the fast cold mode to the slow hot mode driven by the radiative cooling of heated CGM. We consider here the hypothesis that the cold and hot modes promote the formation of thick and thin disks, respectively. Previous theoretical studies showed that this hypothesis explains the age and chemical bimodality of the Milky Way disk as well as the mass dependence of disk duality observed for local galaxies. Using simple galaxy evolution models, we show that this hypothesis also reproduces the thick-to-thin disk mass ratios observed for high-redshift galaxies up to $z=2$. Earlier transition to dual disks in more massive galaxies is explained as the outcome of earlier crossing of the critical halo mass by more massive halos. The caveat here is that this interpretation is not applicable to bulge-dominated galaxies at the high-mass end because present models do not include galaxy mergers, which likely act as the primary route to bulge formation. It should also be noted that the link between the disk duality and gas accretion modes may be indirect and multiple processes may transform accreted material into different types of disks in real galaxies.

papers.title: Kepler Image-Subtracted Light Curves and Variable Star Catalog of NGC 6819
papers.summary: Variable stars in open clusters are valuable probes of stellar evolution. They provide precise measurements of stellar properties, constrain cluster ages and distances, and trace the angular momentum evolution of stellar populations. To advance these studies, we applied image subtraction and systematic reduction techniques to the NGC 6819 Kepler superstamp time-series data (Quarters 1-16), using the Gaia DR3 catalog to identify the positions of individual sources. We produce 81,498 high-precision light curves for 11,055 sources in the crowded field harboring the 2.5 Gyr open cluster NGC 6819. Our detrended light curves achieve a best root-mean-square precision of 33 ppm (6.5-hour bins) for stars with Kepler magnitudes 12 - 12.5 mag, falling to 79 ppm at 14 - 14.5 mag. Using Gaia DR3 proper motions, and parallaxes, we distinguished likely cluster members from field stars. We identified and classified 87 periodic variables that are potential members of NGC 6819, including 26 newly-discovered variables. We make our light curves and variable classifications publicly available to enable further studies of stellar variability and angular momentum evolution in this intermediate-aged open cluster.

papers.title: The MASSIVE SURVEY XXI: Local Variations in the Stellar Initial Mass Function of MASSIVE Early-Type Galaxies
papers.summary: Extensive evidence suggests that the stellar initial mass function (IMF) varies among early-type galaxies (ETGs), but spatially resolved studies within individual galaxies are limited in sample size. We investigate radial variations in the low-mass ($\leq1M_{\odot}$) IMF and its connection to stellar populations in 37 nearby massive ETGs from the MASSIVE survey. Using high-quality Magellan/LDSS-3 long-slit spectroscopy spanning $0.4μ$m$-1.01μ$m, we extract spectra in radial bins reaching outermost radii of 0.2-1.1Re across the sample. We find that the IMF becomes less bottom-heavy with increasing radius in most galaxies. The sample-averaged IMF mismatch parameter, $α_{\rm IMF}=(M/L)/(M/L)_{\rm Kroupa}$, decreases from 2.16 within Re/8 to 1.74 in the Re/4-Re/2 bin, with galaxy-to-galaxy scatters of 0.50 and 0.42, respectively. Thus, the average IMF remains more bottom-heavy than Kroupa and approximately Salpeter-like or more bottom-heavy over these radii. The radial gradients of $\log(α_{\rm IMF})$ anti-correlate with the central value of $α_{\rm IMF}$, indicating that galaxies with more bottom-heavy central IMFs decline more steeply toward less bottom-heavy, approximately Salpeter-like values at larger radii. We find mild positive local correlations between $α_{\rm IMF}$ and stellar metallicity, but no significant local correlation with [Mg/Fe] or [Na/Fe]. Together with the approximately flat profiles of several [$α$/Fe], this suggests that IMF variation in massive ETGs is more closely linked to metallicity than to the star-formation timescale traced by [$α$/Fe]. Finally, the radial variation in stellar $M/L_r$ is dominated by the IMF gradient rather than by the stellar-population gradient. A fixed Kroupa IMF underestimates stellar masses by factors of 1.7 and 1.5 within Re/2 and Re in massive ETGs.

papers.title: Cosmological constraints from the DESI DR1 Bispectrum Full-Shape and DR2 BAO
papers.summary: We present cosmological constraints from the combination of DESI DR1 full-shape measurements, including for the LRG bispectrum, and DESI DR2 BAO data. papers.summary: Dense star clusters are a well-established environment for the formation of gravitational wave sources through dynamical interactions. We also show that high-metallicity cluster models produce low-mass hierarchical mergers consistent with the mass ratios and component masses of GW241011 and GW241110, motivating the importance of high-metallicity clusters in the astrophysical interpretation of future LVK catalogs.

papers.title: Multi-peaked high-frequency gravitational waves from PBH-assisted leptogenesis
papers.summary: We study the possibility of probing non-thermal leptogenesis with multi-peaked high-frequency gravitational waves (GW) by considering heavy right-handed neutrino (RHN) produced from primordial black hole (PBH) evaporation to be responsible for generating the required lepton asymmetry. The decay of RHN also produces a GW spectrum due to graviton bremsstrahlung with the corresponding amplitude being enhanced for heavier RHN. The presence of an ultra-light PBH dominated epoch not only ensures sufficient production of RHNs, but also keeps the leptogenesis scenario free from strong washout problem of thermal leptogenesis at very high scale. In addition, the PBH dominated epoch also helps in generating a gravitational bremsstrahlung spectrum distinct from the stochastic GW background from the thermal bath. Finally, PBH evaporation also brings two separate sources of GW via density perturbation and graviton emission via Hawking evaporation. For the most optimistic scenario with very high scale seesaw consistent with neutrino mass and leptogenesis, this leads to a multi-peaked GW spectrum with peak frequencies lying in the MHz-EHz range.

papers.title: First Results from the LSST Shadow Survey: The Restless Luminous Blue Variable AT2017des in the Virgo-Cluster Galaxy, NGC4532
papers.summary: The Legacy Survey of Space and Time (LSST) will start in late-summer 2026, revolutionizing transient astronomy. Here, we present the Dark Energy Camera (DECam) Shadow Survey, which is designed to maximize the science potential of LSST by shadowing LSST observations of local galaxy-cluster fields, producing a nightly cadence of these fields. The Shadow Survey will discover extremely young supernovae (SNe), SN precursors, as well as other explosive transients and exotic phenomena, helping to characterize such transients at unprecedented cadence and depth when combined with LSST. We describe our workflow, pipeline, public data releases, and candidate vetting. As an early result of Shadow, we present the fitful luminous blue variable (LBV) eruptions of AT2017des in the Virgo-Cluster galaxy NGC4532. AT2017des has short-timescale variability (of order 10 days), peaking at around $M_r=-12.5$mag, brighter than normal LBVs, and similar to the more extreme flaring of hot LBVs/SN impostors such as SN2000ch, AT2016blu, and the precursor activity of SN2009ip. Our spectral time-series reveals features typical of these hot LBVs and SN impostors/precursors. Combining our data with long-baseline photometry from additional observatories, we find that the peaks of the outbursts of AT2017des are getting brighter over time, with 2026 peak fluxes being up to 5 times greater than in 2023 and an average brightening of $\sim0.05$ mag yr$^{-1}$. The peaks of AT2017des are more luminous than those of most other LBVs, only being fainter than bright precursors such as SN2009ip, and extreme SN impostors such as AT2016blu. AT2017des may therefore be ``ramping up'' to a terminal explosion.

papers.title: Wedge-avoidance Fisher Forecasts for Primordial Non-Gaussianity from Dark-Ages 21-cm Power Spectrum and Bispectrum
papers.summary: The Dark Ages offer a uniquely clean window on primordial physics, making the redshifted 21-cm signal a promising probe of primordial non-Gaussianity (PNG). Forecasts for interferometric 21-cm measurements must account for foreground wedge avoidance, which removes a substantial fraction of Fourier modes, and this effect is especially severe at high redshifts. We develop a wedge-aware Fisher framework in which this mode loss is propagated directly into the variances of the cylindrical 21-cm power spectrum and reduced bispectrum. As a case study, we apply the method to forecast PNG constraints in two inflation models with different oscillatory features, a resonant model and a step model, for a lunar far-side array including thermal noise. Since these features affect both the power spectrum and the bispectrum, we apply wedge avoidance to both forecasts and compare their constraining power on PNG amplitudes in these oscillatory feature models. We find that wedge avoidance reduces available Fourier modes significantly in both observables, leading to much weaker constraints on PNG. This framework is broadly applicable to 21-cm power spectrum and bispectrum forecasts across redshifts and is particularly useful in regimes where foreground wedge effect is severe, such as the epoch of reionization and the higher-redshift Dark Ages.

papers.title: Spatially resolved star formation history of Sextans dSph
papers.summary: We present a spatially resolved archaeological reconstruction of the Sextans dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxy using deep DECam wide-field photometry and the PANCAKE CMD-fitting code. By analyzing the star formation history (SFH) and age-metallicity relationship (AMR) across four radial zones, namely: core, ring, outer body, and outskirts, we find that Sextans is a composite system formed through a minor merger approximately 13 Gyr ago. Our results reveal an inverse metallicity gradient: a primitive, metal-poor host (the current core) surrounded by a more massive, chemically evolved envelope ($Δ$[Fe/H] $\approx$ -0.5 dex) introduced by the accreted satellite. We identify a distinct delayed onset of star formation in the ring at $\sim$ 13 Gyr, marking the merger event. While the core quenched early, star formation in the outer body and ring persisted until $\sim$9 Gyr, suggesting that the final cessation of activity was driven by environmental stripping during infall into the Milky Way halo. We propose a plausible scenario to reconcile the derived inverse metallicity gradient with the observed horizontal-branch (HB) morphology and reported Mg deficits. We suggest that the red HB dominance in the core reflects its ancient, $α$-rich nature, while the blue HB in the outskirts represents an $α$-poor, accreted component. However, we note that our CMD-derived [Fe/H] values are model-dependent inferences based on the total metal content $Z$. These findings suggest a non-monolithic assembly for Sextans, posing a testable prediction of a strong radial gradient in [$α$/Fe].

papers.title: Abundances of CNO in candidate young metal-poor stars
papers.summary: In this contribution we investigate the CNO abundances in five apparently young evolved stars, with the aim of discriminating between truly young stars and stars that were rejuvenated by accreting mass from another star. Stars that have accreted mass are expected to show low C and O and a very low [C/O] ratio, as displayed by some stars in the Globular Cluster 47 Tuc, that are believed to have undergone mass-transfer. In our sample the low [C/O] ratios observed appear to be compatible with their evolutionary status. There is thus no indication for these stars having accreted mass.__

papers.title: Constraints on Horndeski Gravity with Phantom Crossing
papers.summary: Gravity models in which the dark energy equation of state crosses $w=-1$, also known as the phantom divide, have received extensive interest due to recent analyses favouring this behaviour. We introduce a new subclass of Horndeski scalar-tensor models capable of generating phantom crossing, whilst remaining minimally coupled to matter: the Asymptotic Cubic Galileon (ACG) models. We show that ACG models can jointly fit the expansion history inferred from observations of the Planck cosmic microwave background, baryon acoustic oscillation measurements from the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument, and distance-ladder supernovae measurements from the Dark Energy Survey. We then demonstrate that perturbative observables, including the galaxy-ISW cross-correlation and void force profile, provide powerful constraints that confine viable and testable ACG models to a well-defined region of the broader Horndeski landscape. Model comparison metrics, including $χ^{2}$ and Bayesian evidence, favour both ACG and $w_{0}w_{a}$CDM models over $Λ$CDM, with ACG providing a fit of comparable quality to $w_{0}w_{a}$CDM. Crucially, ACG models ground the observationally preferred $w_{0}w_{a}$CDM behaviour in a robust Lagrangian formulation. This enables interpretation beyond mere phenomenological fits, and motivates further tests of these models on nonlinear scales.

papers.title: The explosive growth of the Messier 74 galaxy. A galaxy doubling its size in less than a Gigayear
papers.summary: Galaxy formation models predict that galaxies grow inside-out, becoming larger over time. While observations broadly support this paradigm, the nature and timescales of this growth remain poorly constrained. We report the discovery of an extremely faint and young (~600 Myr) stellar component in the outer regions of the nearby galaxy Messier 74 (M74). Using deep optical imaging from the TST telescope at the Teide Observatory, reaching surface brightness limits of ~30-31.5 mag arcsec^-2 in the g, r and i bands, we detect stellar emission extending well beyond the previously known disc radius of ~14 kpc. This newly identified component reaches galactocentric distances of ~30 kpc, effectively doubling the known size of the stellar disc and matching the extent of the HI disc. The revised size of M74 places it in the upper envelope of the mass-size relations. The young age of the outer stellar population suggests a recent episode of disc growth, potentially occurring on timescales shorter than ~1 Gyr. We discuss a possible scenario in which a past flyby interaction with UGC 1176 may have triggered this extended star formation. Further studies of galaxies with similar deep imaging will be key to determining whether such rapid outer disc growth is common or exceptional.

papers.title: Cosmological history after higher dimensional inflation
papers.summary: It was proposed that extra dimensions can acquire large size by higher dimensional inflation connecting two large hierarchies in particle physics and cosmology, namely the weakness of the actual gravitational force to the largeness of the observable universe, in terms of one fundamental scale. This proposal is consistent with the observed approximate scale invariant power spectrum of primordial density perturbations only for one or two extra dimensions of around the micron size. Assuming a stabilisation mechanism of the extra dimensions at the end of inflation, here we propose a cosmological history that describes the Universe evolution after the end of inflation up to the reheating temperature, that guarantees the absence of bulk gravitons at earlier times, avoiding their overproduction in the early universe. The proposed cosmological history connects the period of higher dimensional inflation to the beginning of the standard cosmology.

papers.title: The Hubble tension: A decade review
papers.summary: Ever since the new millennium, precision cosmology has forged the $Λ$-cold-dark-matter ($Λ$CDM) model as the standard model of concordant cosmology, withstanding various tests except for an ever-enlarging discrepancy between early-Universe observations and late-Universe measurements on the current Hubble expansion rate of our observable Universe. This Hubble-constant tension has likely become a real crisis for modern cosmology, with the discrepancy persisting regardless of whether the early-Universe observations depend on \textit{Planck} CMB or not, and the late-Universe measurements depend on distance ladders at all. If the Hubble tension originates from a different early Universe, its resolutions pertain to shrinking the sound horizon by altering either early expansion or recombination histories, but at the same time necessitating modifications to both primordial and late Universe altogether. Alternatively, if the Hubble tension arises from a different late Universe, its resolutions operate by changing the absolute magnitude of supernovae either intrinsically or effectively, both of which have been strongly constrained by the inverse distance ladders with the cosmic distance duality relation. The remaining options seem to turn to our local Universe, but a local Hubble bubble or cosmic void solution has long been ruled out as a significant contribution to the Hubble tension. In view of this dilemma, we review in this paper alternative resolutions involving interacting dark energy models, either combining early-time and late-time modifications or operating at the transition from inhomogeneity to homogeneity scales.

papers.title: New Gauge Forces, Neutron Stars and Schwinger Neutrino Production
papers.summary: We investigate neutrino effects of new long-range forces arising from gauging $B-L$, $L_e-L_{μ/τ}$ or $L_μ-L_τ$ symmetries of the Standard Model. The leptonic potential generated by astronomical bodies, such as the Earth, the Sun or a neutron star, results in the Schwinger pair production of neutrinos charged under the new gauge symmetry. The oppositely charged particles accumulate in the potential well forming a degenerate Fermi gas, while equally charged particles fly away forming a steady flux of neutrinos. We find that, for the $B-L$ and $L_e-L_{μ/τ}$ forces, these effects are too weak to be observable. For the $L_μ-L_τ$ force these effects are significant in neutron stars if the gauge coupling is $g\gtrsim 10^{-18}$. The muonic force changes the element abundances of a neutron star in equilibrium and suppresses its $L_μ-L_τ$ charge. This invalidates the constraint on $g$ from neutron star mergers, at $g\gtrsim 10^{-17}$. Furthermore, for such values of $g$, the neutrino flux produced by the Schwinger effect could potentially be detected from a single young neutron star at a distance of $\simeq 100$ pc, with the typical neutrino energy $E_ν\sim 100$ MeV. A dedicated search for such a signal will reassert the bound $g\lesssim 10^{-18}$.

papers.title: Abundances of CNO in candidate young metal-poor stars
papers.summary: In this contribution we investigate the CNO abundances in five apparently young evolved stars, with the aim of discriminating between truly young stars and stars that were rejuvenated by accreting mass from another star. Stars that have accreted mass are expected to show low C and O and a very low [C/O] ratio, as displayed by some stars in the Globular Cluster 47 Tuc, that are believed to have undergone mass-transfer. In our sample the low [C/O] ratios observed appear to be compatible with their evolutionary status. There is thus no indication for these stars having accreted mass.__

papers.title: Evolving Dark Energy Is Vacuum Energy After All
papers.summary: We investigate a physically motivated model of dynamical dark energy arising from the non-perturbative topological structure of the Quantum Chromodynamics (QCD) vacuum. Unlike conventional dark-energy scenarios, the model does not introduce any new fundamental field or propagating degree of freedom. Instead, the dark-energy density emerges as a global vacuum effect associated with the response of the QCD vacuum to an expanding spacetime, representing a possible paradigm shift in the interpretation of cosmic acceleration. We develop the first comprehensive cosmological implementation of this QCD-induced dark-energy scenario and confront it with current observations, including the latest combination of Planck, ACT and SPT-3G cosmic microwave background measurements, DESI DR2 baryon acoustic oscillation data, and Type Ia supernova samples from Pantheon+ and DES-Dovekie. We compare the model with both the standard $Λ$CDM cosmology and the widely used CPL ($w_0w_a$CDM) parametrization of evolving dark energy. We find that the model provides an excellent fit to the data and reproduces the late-time dark-energy evolution preferred by DESI observations. The inferred cosmological parameters are robust against different implementations of the dark-energy activation mechanism, indicating that the cosmological predictions are largely insensitive to the specific form of the transition. The model naturally predicts an effective phantom-crossing behaviour at intermediate redshifts while remaining free from the theoretical instabilities commonly associated with phantom scalar-field models. Using a combination of goodness-of-fit statistics and Bayesian model-selection techniques, including Akaike and Deviance Information Criteria and Bayesian evidence estimated from Markov Chain Monte Carlo chains, [abridged]

papers.title: The stability of voids in the local Universe: the role of the cosmological constant
papers.summary: The Vlasov kinetic formalism is employed to study the evolution and stability of cosmic voids in the local Universe, taking into account not only gravitational attraction but also the repulsive effect of the cosmological constant (local dark energy). In accordance with the theorem on the general function for the identity between the gravitational fields of a sphere and a point mass, the cosmological constant provides a natural explanation for the Hubble tension as arising due to local and global flows characterized by different Hubble parameters. The crucial role of the Λ-repulsion in maintaining the stability of voids at the present epoch is demonstrated when Landau damping suppresses discrete collapse modes and prevents random local density perturbations inside the voids from growing and involving new galaxies to the walls. Inside the voids, the Λ-repulsion exceeds the attractive force of the residual matter, driving matter outward and accelerating its migration toward the void boundaries. In the local (late) Universe, cosmic voids have entered a stage characterized by stable and more pronounced walls, as can be studied by observational surveys across different redshift ranges.

papers.title: Large primordial non-Gaussianity from transient turns in Higgs-$R^2$ inflation
papers.summary: 本文研究了多场Higgs-R²暴胀中瞬态转弯轨迹对原初非高斯性的影响，发现瞬态转弯能有效将等曲率扰动转移到绝热扇区，产生显著的局域非高斯性，为探测Higgs非最小耦合参数提供了敏感探针。

papers.title: Nonminimal couplings and preheating effects in $R^2$-Higgs inflation after ACT and SPT
papers.summary: 本文研究了R²-希格斯暴胀模型中非最小希格斯-曲率耦合的高维算子，发现dimension-six算子可以解释ACT和SPT观测偏好的标量谱指数增强，并同时通过Goldstone模式生产实现快速预加热，有助于匹配暴胀能标与CMB参考能标。

papers.title: The Small-scale Structures in the Wind of Messier 82
papers.summary: 利用HST窄带成像对M82星系南侧风的分辨率观测，揭示了温暖电离气体在秒差距尺度上的纤维状结构，并发现弥漫性成分随高度增加占主导，线比诊断表明星暴光致电离与激波加热共同作用。

papers.title: MusE GAs FLOw and Wind (MEGAFLOW) XIV: Background-Galaxy Absorption Reveals Kiloparsec-Scale Structure in the Cool Circumgalactic Medium
papers.summary: 本文通过对比背景星系（尺寸几kpc）和类星体（尺寸<0.01 pc）的MgII吸收线堆叠分析，发现中等和平均等值宽度在前者中基本一致，而在后者中显著不同，从而推断CGM中冷云团的相干尺度约为2-7 kpc。

papers.title: AGN-driven BBH mergers: Black hole populations and hierarchical growth across the AGN parameter space
papers.summary: 本文提出了一个更新的半解析框架，用于研究活动星系核（AGN）盘中双黑洞（BBH）的形成与演化，系统探索了AGN参数对并合种群的影响，发现AGN盘可以产生重复并合，形成高质量尾端，产生与GW190521和GW231123等事件一致的系统。

papers.title: Star-formation variability on the star-forming main sequence during the Epoch of Reionization
papers.summary: 本文利用功率谱密度（PSD）模型分析再电离时期（红移z=3-8）星系恒星形成的变化性，发现~10-30 Myr的特征变化时标与星系动力学和恒星反馈时标一致，且低质量星系恒星形成更剧烈、变化更快。

papers.title: Pressure-regulated feedback-modulated star formation as a subgrid model for galaxy formation simulations
papers.summary: 本文提出了一种基于压强调控、反馈调节（PRFM）理论的星际气体演化亚格子模型，并在Arepo移动网格代码中实现了容积版（PRFM-vol）和积分版（PRFM-int）两种版本。相比IllustrisTNG的经验公式，该模型在高压高密度区域气体消耗时间更短，在高分辨率下与TIGRESS模拟的恒星形成率高度一致，且PRFM-int在所有测试分辨率下均保持稳健，为未来宇宙学应用提供了物理基础坚实的恒星形成框架。

papers.title: The dynamics of the Anglerfish cluster
papers.summary: 本文通过多波段观测（X射线、射电、光学）分析了星系团MACS0600的并合动力学，发现一个致密冷核穿越了更大质量的主星系团，并显著扰动了周围簇内介质。

score_breakdown.label: cluster gas rather than high-z galaxy gas

papers.title: Milky Way's warped disc traced by AGB stars
papers.summary: 本文利用富碳（C-rich）和富氧（O-rich）的渐近巨星分支（AGB）星作为示踪体，揭示了银河系翘曲结构，并发现不同年龄的AGB星所呈现的翘曲幅度存在差异，为恒星年龄与翘曲幅度的关系提供了新约束。

papers.title: Star Formation Drives Production of Low Energy Cosmic Rays
papers.summary: 本文利用FAST望远镜对猎户座区域的高保真HI观测，基于HI窄自吸收（HINSA）方法获得了低能宇宙射线（LECR）电离率的高空间分辨率测量，发现LECR电离率与局部恒星形成率（SFR）呈正相关，并且超过外部传播模型的预测值，表明LECR是由恒星形成活动原位产生的，而非来自银河宇宙射线背景。

papers.title: XRISM Observations of Abell 1795: Evidence for Low Turbulence and Resonant Scattering
papers.summary: 本研究利用XRISM/Resolve对星系团Abell 1795进行了高分辨率X射线光谱分析，揭示了视线速度色散、 bulk速度、非热压比等径向分布特征，支持AGN抬升模型而非冷却尾流模型，并发现了多相气体和Fe XXV谱线的共振抑制与过量发射。

score_breakdown.label: cluster gas rather than high-z galaxy gas

papers.title: A Unified Ionization Framework for the Spectroscopic Diversity of Tidal Disruption Events
papers.summary: 本文首次提出了一个统一辐射传输模型，成功再现了光学潮汐瓦解事件（TDEs）的全部四种光谱分类。研究发现，TDE光谱多样性主要由气体电离状态（受注入光度与包层质量之比控制）决定，而极宽的发射线主要源于光学厚外流中的电子散射。该模型解释了光谱类型随电离度降低的自然演化序列（从无特征→He主导→Bowen主导→H主导），并阐明了谱线特征与光度、黑洞质量等物理参数之间的关联。

papers.title: Reconciling large-scale Lyman-$α$ correlations with the SCRIPT Semi-numerical Model
papers.summary: 本文通过扩展SCRIPT半数值再电离模型生成的模拟光谱，发现高红移莱曼α森林观测中大尺度相关性主要由罕见的、高透过率的视线对驱动。通过插入这类视线对，与观测一致的模型比例从17.5%提升至74.1%，表明宇宙方差和稀有视线统计影响可调和现有模型与观测之间的差异。

papers.title: Rapid intermediate-mass black hole formation via runaway mergers of black holes
papers.summary: 本文通过N体模拟发现，在密度≥5×10⁹ M⊙/pc³的致密恒星质量黑洞团中，引力波驱动的黑洞双星连锁并合可在10 Myr内产生约10³ M⊙的中等质量黑洞（IMBH），为高红移超大质量黑洞的快速形成提供了解释。

papers.title: TYC 170-1218-1: A new r-process-enhanced extremely metal-poor star, rich in Th
papers.summary: 本文研究了极端贫金属星TYC 170-1218-1，发现其铁丰度[Fe/H] = -3.52，属于r-II星（[Eu/Fe] = +1.84），富含α元素，贫碳，动力学上属于银河系晕，起源于Sequoia吸积事件。

papers.title: The complex kinematics of the young stars orbiting the supermassive black hole in the Galactic center can be explained by the presence of an intermediate mass companion of Sgr A$^\star$
papers.summary: 本文构建了一个统一模型，综合考虑了恒星的相互作用、共生的星周盘以及一个独立的中等质量伴星（可能是一个中等质量黑洞），以解释围绕银河系中心Sgr A*超大质量黑洞的三个恒星族群（S星、顺时针旋转星和盘外星）的共存与轨道演化。研究表明，只有在中等质量伴星的长期扰动和恒星在气体盘消退环境中的共振弛豫共同作用下，这些恒星当前多样化的轨道才能在数百万年的寿命内同步形成。

papers.title: From Cosmic Web to Supernova Remnants: Modeling FRB DM to Trace Baryons across Multiple Scales
papers.summary: 本论文利用宇宙学模拟、zoom-in星系模拟和超新星遗迹模拟，系统研究了快速射电暴（FRB）色散测量（DM）从宇宙网到源区尺度的多成分贡献，揭示了IGM、CGM、宿主星系及源区环境对DM信号的综合影响。

papers.title: Angular momentum evolution in the CIELO simulations. I. Temporal evolution of gas-stellar misalignments and baryonic merger timing
papers.summary: 利用CIELO模拟追踪了44个星系从z=3.5到z=0的动力学演化，发现近80%的星系在z=0时处于对齐状态，但86%经历过至少一次不对齐阶段。气体-恒星动力学不对齐的根本驱动因素是气体库竞争，而非并合本身。

papers.title: The accretion history of the Milky Way V. The kinematics of most globular clusters trace the merger epochs
papers.summary: 本文通过N体模拟分析球状星团与银河系吸积事件（GSE）的关联，发现盘状起源的GCs会因轨道能量损失而难以示踪吸积事件，而晕族和合并过程中形成的GCs则能保留轨道能量，支持了基于能量-角动量和年龄-金属丰度关系的观测关联。

papers.title: Evolution of the stellar mass function in open clusters from a universal and unsegregated initial state
papers.summary: 本文通过贝叶斯前向建模分析163个高保真度疏散星团，发现年轻星团（<3亿年）的初始质量函数斜率平均为-2.29（与Salpeter斜率一致，固有弥散0.17），且在气体耗尽初期（约1千万年）几乎不存在质量分层。研究表明动力学演化存在不同时标：质量分层通过内部弛豫快速进行，而潮汐蒸发导致的全局MF平坦化在约6亿年后才占主导。

papers.title: A linear residual structure across galaxy rotation curves
papers.summary: 通过分析SPARC和LITTLE THINGS巡天的星系，发现扣除重子物质贡献后的残差呈现共同的线性模式，并分为质量耦合和质量无关两种成分。

papers.title: Bulk vs. turbulent motions at the centres of galaxy clusters: AGN-driven turbulence according to TNG-Cluster
papers.summary: 本文利用TNG-Cluster模拟数据，分析了352个星系团中热气体的湍流运动，发现大部分ICM表现为亚声速湍流，但速度分布范围很广，中心区域湍流速度弥散度约为50-75 km/s，SMBH反馈是驱动团核湍流的关键机制。

score_breakdown.label: cluster gas rather than high-z galaxy gas

papers.title: The quenching time and timescale distribution of z~2 quiescent galaxies from precise colour distribution analysis
papers.summary: 本文利用模拟推断方法，通过宽波段测光颜色分布直接约束红移1.7-2之间大质量星系的形成时间、熄灭时间和熄灭时标，发现星系必须快速熄灭（中位时标约182百万年）才能实现精确的双峰颜色分布。

papers.title: A New Member of the Fast and Furious Family: A Relativistic and Time-Variable UV Outflow in a Luminous Quasar
papers.summary: 在类星体SDSS J231854.31+243954.2中首次探测到紫外波段的最快外流，速度达-77,000至-90,000 km/s（约0.3c），其动能光度比下限超过反馈阈值，对宿主星系具有显著反馈效应。

papers.title: A Decade to Map the Diffuse Universe: FRB-QSO Pairs with HST/COS Spectroscopy
papers.summary: 本文预测到2035年，下一代干涉仪将探测到数千个快速射电暴（FRB）与紫外亮类星体（QSO）的投影对，利用HST/COS联合分析可约束宇宙弥散介质的电离分数、重子质量、磁场结构和湍流，并绘制多相银河系及M31晕的三维地图。

papers.title: The Extreme Rarity and Physical Properties of Low-redshift AGNs with Balmer Absorption
papers.summary: 本文通过对14584个低红移活动星系核的均匀光谱分析，发现仅7个源（~0.05%）具有稳健的巴尔默吸收线特征，并利用部分覆盖吸收体模型进行建模。其中类LRD源J1025的覆盖因子（Cf≥0.8）与高红移LRD一致。研究发现三个具有极端弱Fe II发射、高爱丁顿比和低金属丰度的源，其巴尔默吸收发生率可升至~10%，表明低金属丰度可能抑制盘风，有助于在高吸积率系统中保留沿视线方向的致密中性气体。

papers.title: The Origin of Da Scaling: Suppressed Cooling in Fast-Cooling Mixing Layers
papers.summary: 本文研究了湍流辐射混合层（TRML）中当冷却时间极短（Damköhler数很大）时，总辐射能量标度律从 $\dot{E}_{\rm cool} \propto {\rm Da}^{1/2}$ 转变为 $\dot{E}_{\rm cool} \propto {\rm Da}^{1/4}$ 的机制，发现这是由于流入气体的冲压抑制了湍流界面的分形折叠所致。

papers.title: Ceci n'est pas une Couche de Mélange: The Meaning of Resolved Turbulent Radiative Mixing
papers.summary: 本文研究了湍流辐射混合层（TRMLs）的分辨率问题，发现总冷却率的分辨率无关性源于数值耗散与数值粘度的偶然抵消，并提出了“湍流菲尔德长度”作为正确解析相结构的关键尺度。

papers.title: Mapping the nuclear environments of extreme coronal line emitting galaxies
papers.summary: 本文研究了33个极端日冕发射线天体（ECLEs），将其分为与潮汐瓦解事件（TDE）相关的变源和与活动星系核（AGN）相关的非变源。通过多历元光谱分析，发现变源中高电离谱线先出现后消失，揭示了电离连续谱软化和电离前沿传播的物理过程。同时建立了发射线气体距离与黑洞质量的正相关关系。

papers.title: Studying the absorption signatures of H I Lyman-α in the warm-hot circumgalactic medium with TNG50
papers.summary: 本研究利用TNG50宇宙学模拟，发现围绕z=0银河系质量星系的热暖星系周介质（CGM）存在一种名为日冕宽Lyα吸收体（CBLA）的HI吸收特征，其覆盖分数高达约0.8，贡献了CGM中约50%的HI吸收，并追踪了温度在10^5.2-6.4 K的热暖气体，占星系总重子预算约7%。

papers.title: Beyond Self-Similarity: Reconciling X-Ray Scaling Relations in Galaxy Clusters and Groups
papers.summary: 本文通过引入两个幂律模型参数（气体质量分数 $f_g$ 和温度变化 $f_T$），有效地描述了星系团和星系群在X射线标度关系中偏离自相似模型的现象，并通过MCMC元分析校准了这些参数，显著提高了预测精度，将高度偏离的标度关系比例从49%降至11%。

papers.title: Constraints on the gravitational potential from DESI DR2 BAO and its implications for the local void scenario
papers.summary: 本文研究了宇宙局部空洞对哈勃常数张力的影响，发现即使考虑空洞导致的重力红移效应，标准ΛCDM模型仍优于空洞模型，但空洞模型并不能被高红移数据完全排除。

papers.title: A New Record Census of Dwarf AGN and a Bimodal $M_{\rm BH}$-$M_{\star}$ Scaling Relation with DESI DR1
papers.summary: 利用DESI DR1光谱数据，在1678,787个低红移线发射星系中识别AGN，发现AGN比例随恒星质量单调增加，并将$M_{\rm BH} - M_{\star}$标度关系延伸至低质量端，同时发布了包含约740万个星系的EmFit增值星表。

papers.title: Revisiting ram pressure stripping in Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte: No evidence for stripped HI with LGLBS
papers.summary: 对WLM矮星系的HI 21厘米观测显示，此前MeerKAT数据中发现的星系外气体可能源于观测误差，而非真实存在的冲压剥离现象。

papers.title: Ultraviolet diversity of Little Red Dots as a probe for direct-collapse black hole ages
papers.summary: 本文利用MELIORA宇宙学流体动力学模拟，研究了红移z>8.5的小红点（LRDs）中PopIII恒星和直接塌缩黑洞（DCBHs）的贡献。发现宿主星系的紫外辐射在DCBH形成后约3000万年内快速演化，从初始的黑洞主导系统转变为恒星主导系统。UV明亮的LRDs倾向于拥有更年长的DCBHs、更高的金属丰度、更低的黑洞-恒星质量比和更低的爱丁顿比。LRD相位的短暂性对其宇宙学出现时间施加强约束。

papers.title: Understanding the Broad-line Region of Active Galactic Nuclei with Photoionization. II. Slim disks, Self-shadowing, and BLR sizes
papers.summary: 本文通过分析吸积盘结构、宽线区云团性质及光致电离过程，发现高吸积率的活动星系核（AGN）在宽线区半径-光度关系上出现偏离，主要由内盘自遮光效应和吸积率依赖的宽线区气体密度增加共同导致。

papers.title: Interacting dark energy constraints from Fermi GRBs and Pantheon+ SNe Ia with full GRB covariance
papers.summary: 本研究利用15年的Fermi/GBM长伽马暴数据和Pantheon+ Ia型超新星数据，检验相互作用暗能量（IDE）模型是否优于标准ΛCDM模型。结果表明，在考虑完整协方差的情况下，IDE模型并未显著提升拟合优度，贝叶斯信息准则更倾向于简单的ΛCDM模型，当前数据无法为相互作用暗能量提供证据支持。

papers.title: Spectroscopic characterization of Young Stellar Populations and their Feedback in NGC 5253
papers.summary: 本文利用HST/COS的远紫外光谱数据分析了近邻金属贫乏蓝致密矮星系NGC 5253中的年轻大质量星团，并与VLT/MUSE光学观测结合，研究了星团对其周围电离气体外流的影响。研究发现，光谱测定的年龄优先于测光方法，且外流速度与星团年龄、质量及超新星驱动的机械光度之间存在显著相关性。尽管理论预测低金属丰度环境下机械反馈存在延迟，但在NGC 5253（Z~0.3 Z⊙）中，超新星反馈在>5 Myr时已明显显现，未观察到明显延迟。

papers.title: Unveiling the population of massive quenched galaxies at $z\ge2$ in the COLIBRE simulations -- II. The role of AGN feedback and environment on their emergence
papers.summary: 本文利用COLIBRE宇宙学流体动力学模拟，研究早期（z≥2）大质量淬灭星系（MQGs）的形成机制。研究发现AGN反馈是主要淬灭机制，但热模型和混合模型表现不同。MQGs拥有更大质量的黑洞和更高的恒星形成效率，约54%的z=3 MQGs是z=0星系的主要前身，但多数经历再活化事件。

papers.title: MAUVE-MUSE: When Metallicity Follows or Fights Star Formation -- A Mass-Dependent Inversion in Virgo Galaxies
papers.summary: 本文利用MAUVE-MUSE巡天数据分析了14个室女座螺旋星系，在约100pc尺度上研究了金属丰度与恒星形成面密度的关系，发现了一个质量依赖的反转现象：低恒星质量面密度区域呈现反相关，高恒星质量面密度区域呈现正相关，并通过气体调节器模型提供了统一解释。

papers.title: Mapping the Universe as a Bianchi I cosmology with Gaia data
papers.summary: 本研究利用盖亚DR3中120万个类星体的自行数据，通过矢量球谐函数分析检验了轴对称Bianchi I模型（各向异性宇宙模型）的预测，发现信号中存在显著的四极分量，但振幅随红移的增长趋势未被确认，局部膨胀剪切值高于预期。

papers.title: A New Cartwheel-like Collisional Ring Galaxy
papers.summary: 发现了一个新的卡通车式碰撞环星系PGC 1112751，命名为“Eridanus Wheel”（EW），它由星系正面碰撞形成，具有内外环和辐条结构，与著名卡通车星系相似。

papers.title: The incidence and star-formation properties of X-ray active galactic nuclei across the star-forming main sequence in DESI-eRASS1: the role of host-galaxy morphology
papers.summary: 利用DESI-eRASS1数据，研究了1171个X射线AGN和45374个非AGN星系在恒星形成主序上的性质，发现AGN与恒星形成的关系受宿主星系形态（盘状vs球状）和宇宙红移的调控。

papers.title: High-S/N Quasar Observations with HST/COS: Deep Fields for Spectroscopy
papers.summary: 本文提出利用HST/COS对约20个QSO进行深紫外光谱巡天（信噪比>30，分辨率20 km/s），以产生类似哈勃深场（HDF/UDF）的遗产数据集，推动星系周介质（CGM）、星际介质（IGM）、银河系弥散气体及AGN外流等前沿科学研究，并为未来Habitable Worlds Observatory的紫外科学铺路。

papers.title: Reconstructing the Globular Cluster Initial Mass Function from Present-Day Globular Cluster Systems
papers.summary: 本文通过反演动力学质量损失，重建了不同宿主星系质量下球状星团初始质量函数（GCIMF）的高质量端，发现GCIMF呈现幂律分布且斜率与星系质量强相关，表明GCIMF依赖于星系性质。

papers.title: Puzzling Ultra-Diffuse Galaxy Evolution (PUDGE). II. A transformation pathway from UDGs to compact dwarfs (CDs) in galaxy clusters
papers.summary: 利用IllustrisTNG模拟，发现致密矮星系（CDs）的前身星在进入星系团前会经历一个短暂的超扩散星系（UDG）阶段，通过星暴过程在剥离过程中新生恒星核，而非经典潮汐剥离理论所认为的暴露已有恒星核。

papers.title: The Gas-Phase Mass-Metallicity Relation of Dwarf Galaxies Across Large-Scale Environments Using the CAVITY Parent Sample
papers.summary: 本文利用CAVITY巡天样本，系统研究了矮星系在不同宇宙环境（空洞、纤维状结构、星系团）中的气体相位金属丰度-恒星质量关系（MZR）。研究发现MZR斜率随环境变化：空洞中较陡（0.28），星系团中较平缓（0.17），且纤维状结构中孤立与非孤立矮星表现不同。结果表明环境效应是造成矮星系MZR散度的重要因素。

papers.title: Beyond the [OIII]$λ$4363 auroral line: [NeIII]$λ$3868 as a direct tracer of electron temperature
papers.summary: 本文提出了一种利用[NeIII]λ3868和[OIII]λ4959,5007亮线比值(O3Ne3)来估算电子温度的新方法，解决了在低温(高金属丰度)环境下[OIII]λ4363极弱难以探测的问题，适用于6000-13500K的温度范围。

papers.title: Probing the environments of FRI and FRII radio galaxies in LoTSS DR2 with galaxy clusters
authors: J. M. G. H. J. de Jong
papers.summary: 本文利用LoTSS DR2巡天数据与DESI星系团目录交叉匹配，研究FRI/FRII射电星系形态二分法是否与星系团环境有关。结果发现FRII在星系团中出现频率更低，尤其在高射电光度下差异更显著，但一旦进入星系团，两者所处环境相似，说明星系团尺度环境并非二分法的主因。

papers.title: Atomic gas properties at the positions of supernovae Type Ia, II, and Ib/c
papers.summary: 本文基于133颗超新星的样本，分析了其所在位置的原子气体分布，发现II型超新星在原子气体分布上的偏移最大，但所有类型超新星的位置分布与原子气体密度并无确定性关联，无法证实Ib/c和II型超新星与最密集的原子气体有关。

papers.title: The $τ$ of Neutral Hydrogen: Increased CMB Optical Depth at Long Wavelengths
papers.summary: 本文提出利用长波（>21cm）CMB光子被中性氢吸收产生的额外光学深度，通过交叉关联射电波段与毫米波段CMB涨落，来测量宇宙历史中中性氢分数与自旋温度之比（x_HI/T_s），这比直接探测21cm信号更容易实现。

papers.title: Advancing the detection of low surface brightness galaxies. I. ATTILA: multi-tAsking deTecTIon tool for Lsb gAlaxies
papers.summary: 本文介绍了一个名为ATTILA的新型自动检测工具，用于改进超暗星系（UDGs）和低表面亮度（LSB）星系的探测与表征。在Hydra I星系团中，通过VST VEGAS巡天数据，成功识别了24个新的UDGs（使总数翻倍至48个）以及92个额外的LSB星系，检测率超过80%。

papers.title: Powerful Radio Sources in the Southern Sky. IV. Observations of the G4Jy-3CRE Catalog with the Australian Square Kilometre Array Pathfinder
papers.summary: 本文利用ASKAP的RACS巡天数据对G4Jy-3CRE星表（南天最亮射电源）进行了新的连续谱成像，在264个源中识别出173个具有喷流形态的源（其中37个此前未显示喷流活动），并新确定了6个光学对应体。

papers.title: From protogalaxy through thick and thin: Why did the Milky Way evolve in three kinematic phases?
papers.summary: 本文通过FIRE-2模拟发现，银河系结构演化经历了三个运动学阶段：早期无序的原星系、旋转的厚盘阶段、以及冷薄盘阶段，并揭示了各阶段形成的物理机制。

papers.title: Dynamical Cluster Assembly Framework (D-CAF): The Link Between Star Cluster Formation and Expansion Rates
papers.summary: D-CAF框架连接了恒星形成历史与年轻星团的动力学演化，发现气体在恒星形成过程中持续收缩，显著影响星团的存活和膨胀，且膨胀特征仍保留着形成时的动力学信息。

papers.title: Bulgeless Evolution And the Rise of Discs (BEARD) II. The role of mergers in shaping the Milky Way analogues in TNG50
papers.summary: 利用TNG50模拟研究无核星系（B/D<0.08）的形成与演化，发现74%经历过至少一次重大并合，但并合性质（气体丰富、共面、共转）有助于盘结构存活；无核星系形成较晚（z~0.7）、暗晕质量较低、恒星比角动量较高，具有逐渐的盘生长和最小核球生长，恒星种群更年轻。

papers.title: The limits of feedback from active galactic nuclei
papers.summary: 本文利用FLAMINGO模拟研究AGN反馈为何在星系群中显著耗散气体，而在星系团中效果不佳。通过划分三个径向区域（内激波加热区、中间浮力区、外终止激波区），发现加热自限性导致外流具有几乎与晕质量无关的上限熵值（约360 keV cm²），只有当该值高于入流熵时气体才能逃逸，这一条件仅在晕质量小于10^13.7 M⊙时满足，从而解释了群与团的气体含量差异。

papers.title: From inter-filamentary gas to filaments and hubs: gas flows in the Mon R2 hub-filament system
papers.summary: 该研究利用Nobeyama 45米望远镜的$^{13}$CO和C$^{18}$O观测数据，分析了Monoceros R2 Hub-纤维系统(HFS)中低密度和高密度气体的运动学。发现纤维(Fs)中的气体沿纤维方向流向中心的速度远快于纤维间区域(IFs)；同时，至少30%的IFs气体会流向Fs进行物质补充。这表明需同时考虑高低密度气体对中心大质量恒星形成的物质供应。

papers.title: Magnetised CGM Gas at z~1 revealed by SPICE-RACS
papers.summary: 本研究利用SPICE-RACS DR2和DESI DR1数据，构建了包含2483条类星体视线(其中612条含Mg II吸收体)的最大样本，通过扣除银河系贡献后，发现Mg II视线在残余RM色散上比对照样本高出4.13±0.91 rad/m²（4.5σ显著性），表明在红移~1.14处，星系周介质磁场强度已达约0.4-0.8 μG，证明宇宙在z~1时星系晕已建立了显著的磁化环境。

papers.title: Co-evolution of Supermassive Black Holes and their Host L* galaxies: implications for Milky Way and M31
papers.summary: 基于ARTEMIS和EAGLE模拟，研究了L*星系中超大质量黑洞质量离散的起源，发现不同的并合历史导致黑洞质量差异：活跃并合历史产生更大质量黑洞和椭圆星系，宁静历史产生较小黑洞和盘状星系。

papers.title: Observation of spontaneous N-bearing PAH formation using ion trap: a new formation pathway in the interstellar medium
papers.summary: 本研究通过离子阱技术和电子结构计算，揭示了嘧啶阳离子与乙炔之间多个无势垒反应，生成了此前未报道的内环氮掺杂多环芳烃，这对理解土卫六大气和星际介质中的天体化学具有重要意义。

papers.title: Constraining the Galactic Center Dark Cluster with ELT/MICADO Observations
papers.summary: 本文探讨了利用极大望远镜（ELT）及其首光成像仪MICADO系统性地搜索银河系中心致密天体（恒星级黑洞、中子星、白矮星）的可行性与科学意义，旨在首次定量约束"暗星团"的分布与性质。

papers.title: Metal Enrichment by the First Stars Exploding at the Lower Energy Limit of Pair-Instability Supernovae
papers.summary: 本文通过宇宙学流体力学模拟，研究低质量（140 M☉）低能量（5×10^51 erg）的对不稳定超新星（PISN）对第二代恒星金属丰度的影响。发现第二代恒星全部由同一暗晕内的第一代恒星内部富集，其中位[Fe/H]丰度约为-5.5，比高能量PISN情况低2.9 dex。结果表明，如果PISN在早期宇宙中普遍存在，我们应该能在极端贫金属（EMP）恒星中观测到其特征性奇偶丰度模式，而观测中未发现这种模式强烈否定了PISN作为早期金属富集主导渠道的可能性。

papers.title: Unveiling Hidden Lyman Alpha Emitters in the DESI DR1 Data
papers.summary: 本文提出基于CNN的方法，从DESI DR1光谱数据中自动检测Lyman alpha发射体（LAE），模型在12分钟内处理约200万光谱，检测出19,685个LAE（z~2-3.5），纯度95.2%，完备度95.9%。

papers.title: Unmasking Stellar Feedback-Driven Bubbles: Identification and Properties Analysis
papers.summary: 本文利用模拟数据研究类似NGC300的矮旋涡星系中由恒星反馈驱动的热电离气泡的特性与演化，发现气泡寿命和大小呈指数分布，且与星系中心距离呈正相关，并通过Hα示踪剂与观测结果进行比较验证。

papers.title: Physical characterization and modeling of candidate Hyper-Compact HII Regions
papers.summary: 本文利用VLA对5个候选超致密HII区进行了2cm和6cm观测，通过均匀/非均匀球体和风模型分析，发现大多数源为小型弱超致密HII区，仅G40.28-0.22最可能是真正的超致密HII区，部分源也可能是喷流或星风。

papers.title: Co-evolution of the Milky Way high- and low-α sequences with chemical evolution models
papers.summary: 本文通过修订的并行化学演化模型（考虑预富集延迟的第二次内落事件），成功解释了银河系厚盘和薄盘恒星在[α/Fe] vs. [Fe/H]图中的二分性、共演化阶段以及年老低α恒星的存在。

papers.title: A statistical look on kinematic planes of satellite galaxies II: The physics behind their early formation in TNG50 MW/M31-like galaxies
papers.summary: 本文利用TNG50模拟中的190个银河系/仙女座星系-like系统，研究了卫星星系动力学持久平面(KPPs)的物理起源。发现约67%的KPP中卫星轨道极轴与拉格朗日体积的强坍缩方向对齐，且卫星在约40亿年宇宙年龄时快速沉降到共同轨道平面上，支持KPPs是ΛCDM宇宙中高红移各向异性质量坍缩的化石遗迹这一观点。

papers.title: Chemical composition and kinematics of ionised gas in low-mass star-forming galaxies with extremely high [OIII]/[OII] ratios
papers.summary: 本文利用VLT/Xshooter观测了11个低红移(z<0.085)的致密恒星形成星系（高O32样本），这些星系具有极高的[OIII]λ5007/[OII]3727比值（11-42），是潜在的莱曼连续谱辐射泄漏候选体。研究测量了元素丰度及不同发射线的速度弥散，发现Hβ的速度弥散随恒星质量增大和O32比值减小而增加，且不同谱线的速度弥散比值大多接近1，但HeII 4686和HeI 10830显著高于1，而[OII] 3726,3729和[SII] 6717,6731低于1，反映了不同区域电离气体的动力学差异。

papers.title: Differences between emission and absorption tracers of spatially resolved outflows in clumpy z ~ 0.1 star-forming galaxies
papers.summary: 本文通过对三个z~0.1的星暴星系进行空间分辨光谱分析，发现Mg II吸收线测得的外流速度（~-560 km/s）显著高于Hα发射线测得的速度（~-124 km/s），两者存在约0.4 dex的系统性偏移。尽管速度绝对值不同，但外流速度与星系性质（如恒星形成率）的相关性斜率一致。研究认为这种偏移主要源于吸收和发射示踪气体密度的系统性差异，而非电离态不同。

papers.title: Revisiting the Constancy of the Speed of Light: Galaxy Cluster Mass Bias Implications
papers.summary: 本研究利用星系团X射线气体质量分数和Ia型超新星距离数据，结合SH0ES先验和重子-物质密度比，检验光速是否恒定。结果表明，采用CLASH或CCCP标定时未发现光速变化，而Planck标定下光速恒定假设仅以2σ水平勉强成立，结果对标定方案敏感。

papers.title: Investigating the Effects of Bars on Star Formation and Nuclear Activity of Galaxies Using DESI Survey Data
papers.summary: 基于DESI DR1和Galaxy Zoo DESI数据，分析了33,201个盘状星系中棒状结构、恒星形成与活动星系核活动之间的关系。发现强棒多存在于红色、大质量星系，而弱棒常见于蓝色、低质量星系。强棒在低质量星系中促进中心恒星形成，但在大质量星系中抑制比恒星形成率。棒状星系中AGN活动更普遍，但棒的结构参数与AGN活动相关性较弱，表明二者关系是间接的。

papers.title: Built to Rest: The Evolving Star-Forming Main Sequence Requires Episodic Quiescence or Late Assembly
papers.summary: 本文构建了一个解析模型，研究星系沿恒星形成主序的演化历史，发现低质量早期形成星系若始终保持在主序上，最终会演化为今天的大质量星系，而银河系质量的星系必须经历显著的恒星形成抑制阶段，否则会变得过于庞大。

papers.title: A Virgo Environmental Survey Tracing Ionised Gas Emission (VESTIGE). XXI. Statistical properties of individual HII regions in perturbed galaxies
papers.summary: 本文利用VESTIGE巡天的窄带Hα+[NII]成像数据，在室女座星系团的385个星系中识别出76645个HII区，重点研究受扰动的（HI气体缺乏）星系。结果表明，与未受扰动的富气体星系相比，受扰动星系的HII区光度函数具有更陡的暗端斜率和更亮的特征光度，且这种差异主要源于外盘。扰动星系单位恒星质量和盘面面积的HII区数量更少，差异随HI缺乏程度增加而增大，这可用冲压剥离气体导致恒星形成活动由外向内熄灭来解释。

papers.title: Constraints on Halo Gas Profiles from Joint kSZ and Galaxy Clustering Analysis of ACT DR6 and CMASS
papers.summary: 本文通过联合分析成对kSZ效应和星系成团性，利用CMASS星系和ACT DR6巡天数据测量了动力学Sunyaev-Zel'dovich信号，以7.2σ的显著度探测到kSZ信号，并重建了晕光学深度轮廓，发现观测轮廓比模拟更为延展，暗示真实宇宙中重子反馈可能更强。

papers.title: Non-Equilibrium Ionisation in Photoionised Haloes: Implications for Shock Stability and Absorption-Line Signatures
papers.summary: 本文通过1D流体模拟研究了非平衡电离（NEI）和紫外背景对星系周围气体热演化、激波稳定性和吸收特征的影响，发现紫外背景能抑制NEI效应、恢复热平衡，并产生与观测相符的OVI、CIV和HI吸收柱密度，强调需考虑IGM贡献以正确解释CGM吸收线观测。

papers.title: Resolving the Unresolved Galactic Winds in Multi-phase Models. I. Methodology and Application
papers.summary: 本文利用Fielding & Bryan 2022的多相、多尺度风模型，拟合高分辨观测的柱密度轮廓，提取出三个关键流出参数（初始热相质量加载因子、初始冷相质量加载因子、初始冷云质量），发现大多数星系的冷相质量加载因子约为1，且模型能间接约束热风性质，并预测了径向速度和质量加载因子的变化趋势。

papers.title: XMAGNET -- Stir before serving: a Lagrangian perspective on mixing-driven condensation in the intracluster medium
papers.summary: 本文通过磁流体动力学（MHD）和纯流体动力学模拟，研究星系团冷核中冷相气体的形成机制，发现混合驱动的热力学路径是主导冷凝过程，而磁场显著影响冷气体的聚集历史和运动学特性。

score_breakdown.label: cluster gas rather than high-z galaxy gas

papers.title: A Universal Dance of Galactic Disks: Ubiquitous Precession and Its Implications
papers.summary: 利用IllustrisTNG模拟发现，银河系类星系的盘面进动是一种普遍现象，由30 kpc内的非均匀物质分布产生的潮汐力矩驱动，显著影响星系演化，并导致冷气体翘曲、恒星轨道加热及卫星星系径向排列等观测现象。

papers.title: The Critical Mass in Galaxy Evolution
papers.summary: 利用Horizon Run 5模拟，发现星系临界质量（约10^12.5 M⊙）源于热气体晕的形成抑制冷气体流入，导致恒星形成效率下降，从而驱动恒星-总质量比的转折。

papers.title: Turbulence and Star Formation Suppression in Elliptical Galaxies: The Role of Active Galactic Nucleus Jet Wind Interaction
papers.summary: 黑洞吸积率低时，喷流和风共同作用才能有效产生AGN反馈，二者单独均不足以在椭圆星系中产生强湍流和抑制冷气体凝聚。

papers.title: SPYGLASS. VII-B. Tracing the Fragments of Massive Star Formation Using Low-Mass Associations
papers.summary: 利用Gaia数据追踪16个低质量年轻星协的起源，发现其中12个与更大结构相关，揭示了气泡反馈、云碰撞等恒星形成机制，并指出低质量星协对理解星系恒星形成过程至关重要。

papers.title: Coupling between stellar and HI lopsidedness in Milky Way-type galaxies from the Auriga Superstars cosmological simulations
papers.summary: 本文利用Auriga Superstars宇宙学zoom-in模拟，分析了九个银河系类型星系中恒星和中性氢（HI）的不对称性（lopsidedness）。研究发现，老年恒星（>0.5Gyr）与HI在形态不对称性上强相关，反映了整体引力势的扭曲；而年轻恒星（<0.5Gyr）则追踪螺旋臂上的不对称恒星形成。恒星形态与运动学不对称性强相关，但HI的相关性较弱。强棒状星系往往具有更对称的盘和更高的中心恒星质量密度。潮汐相互作用（质量比>1:50）会同时诱发恒星和HI的不对称性，而平滑气体吸积主要影响HI和年轻恒星。

papers.title: Bayesian power spectrum estimation with modelling of systematic effects in delay-fringe rate space
papers.summary: 本文提出了一种在21cm射电干涉测量中，通过对系统效应（特别是电缆反射）进行贝叶斯建模与边缘化，从而恢复再电离时期信号功率谱的方法，并在单基线模拟数据上验证了其有效性。

papers.title: Double-Peaked Ly$α$ Emission during Reionization Requires Nearby Voids and a Favorable Local Ionizing Background
papers.summary: 本文通过宇宙黎明III模拟，发现再电离时期观测到的双峰Lyα发射起源于星系附近数兆秒差距尺度的极端低密度空洞，这些空洞使蓝端光子免受共振散射；双峰发射概率对电离背景强度高度敏感，可作为再电离晚期电离背景的灵敏探针。

papers.title: Blinded Mock Data Challenge: Is the Spectral Siren Technique Robust for Measuring the Hubble Constant?
papers.summary: 本文通过盲测挑战分析表明，利用谱 sirens 技术测量哈勃常数时，必须准确了解双黑洞质量分布随宇宙红移的演化，特别是其与金属丰度依赖性和时间延迟分布的相互作用，否则模型误配将导致哈勃常数和双黑洞群体参数的错误推断。

papers.title: Constraining the nature of active galactic nuclei through circumgalactic Lya emission at z=2-3
papers.summary: 本文分析了59个无遮蔽类星体和26个遮蔽类星体周围的光晕莱曼α星云，发现无遮蔽类星体的星云更不对称、更延展，速度弥散呈陡降趋势（暗示大尺度外流），而遮蔽类星体的星云更对称、更致密，速度弥散平坦。这些发现挑战了传统的基于取向的AGN统一模型，支持了演化论模型——即AGN反馈驱动从遮蔽相向无遮蔽相转变。

papers.title: Bound or blown: the fate of hot gas in galaxy groups
papers.summary: 本文通过将X-GAP观测样本与FLAMINGO流体模拟进行前向建模对比，发现中等强度的AGN反馈模型与观测数据吻合最佳（最低张力仅0.8σ），而极端反馈模型被排除（>4σ），表明星系群热力学性质偏好强于基准但弱于最剧烈喷射的反馈方案。

papers.title: Cosmic gas accretion from filaments onto galaxy clusters using the IllustrisTNG simulation
papers.summary: 本研究利用IllustrisTNG-300模拟分析了415个星系团，揭示了宇宙纤维状结构驱动各向异性气体吸积的两个不同区域：在团内环境（~2-4R200）中，纤维管道输送暖热气体进入团周介质，但纤维本身因热化缓慢而温度较低；在团外围（~1-2R200）中，沿纤维下落的气体进入热团内介质并产生强切向速度梯度。研究发现松驰且大质量的星系团表现出更强的温度不连续性，但未发现纤维周围存在强吸积激波的证据。

score_breakdown.label: cluster gas rather than high-z galaxy gas

papers.title: Reconstructing the cosmic expansion with a generalized q(z) parameterization: A decelerating Universe from late-time constraints
papers.summary: 本文提出了一种广义的、包含有效辐射成分的减速参数化模型 $q(z)$，利用CC、SNIa、HIIG和QSO等多种低中红外观测数据约束参数，得到当前减速参数 $q_0 = -0.25^{+0.04}_{-0.04}$、跃迁红移 $z_T \simeq 0.80$ 及哈勃参数 $h = 0.729 \pm 0.006$，结果表明宇宙当前加速但晚期加速可能减弱，且模型在高红移行为受控、演化平滑。

papers.title: Probing the Hot Gaseous Halos of Milky Way-like Galaxies in the TNG50 simulation
papers.summary: 通过与eROSITA和O VII/O VIII观测数据对比，发现TNG50模拟的MW质量星系热晕在空间上过于致密，且缺乏高温（~10^6.5 K）气体成分，表明反馈模型能量注入过于中心化且剧烈。

papers.title: With arms wide open: a VLT/MUSE view of the mechanisms driving unwinding spiral arms in cluster galaxies
papers.summary: 本文利用VLT/MUSE空间分辨分析，研究了两颗星系UG101和UG103中旋臂解绕的机制，发现UG101主要由潮汐相互作用驱动，UG103则主要由冲压剥离（RPS）驱动，并提供了从空间分辨性质区分驱动机制的分析框架。

papers.title: Orbital evolution of highly eccentric bodies embedded in a ringed accretion disc
papers.summary: 本文研究了在具有密度环的等温盘中，高偏心率低质量点质量吸积体的轨道演化。结果表明，顺行吸积体轨道会逐渐圆化，半长轴向环半径收敛，从而在气态环上叠加形成环状种群分布，即密度环起到了迁移陷阱的作用。逆行吸积体则始终向内迁移。

papers.title: Chaotic migration of LISA Extreme Mass Ratio Inspirals in a turbulent accretion disk: effect on waveform de-phasing
papers.summary: 本文研究LISA引力波探测器观测的极端质量比旋近（EMRI）在湍流吸积盘中的轨道演化，发现当考虑湍流扭矩时，即使线性扭矩导致的引力波相位差不可观测，湍流效应也可能使相位差达到可探测水平，从而激励未来开展含EMRI的吸积盘磁流体动力学模拟。

papers.title: Revisiting radio synchrotron diagnostics in star-forming galaxies
papers.summary: 本文通过MHD模拟和CREST框架，检验了射电连续谱观测中三个常见假设，发现：仅靠宇宙线平流无法解释观测到的垂直射电强度轮廓；射电谱不能直接代表整个星系的CR电子谱；即使损失率不占主导，轫致辐射和库仑损失也不能忽略。

papers.title: Self-regulated galaxy evolution within a self-consistently varying galaxy-wide IMF
papers.summary: 该研究探讨了星系演化半解析模型中，依赖于金属丰度和恒星形成率的星系范围初始质量函数（IGIMF）对恒星形成自调节的影响。研究发现IGIMF模型能更准确地预测质量-金属丰度关系，并为淬灭椭圆星系提供更全面的描述。

papers.title: Unraveling Chemical Enrichment in Extreme Emission-Line Galaxies: A Multi-Element Bayesian View of Bursty Star Formation and Galaxy Evolution in DESI
papers.summary: 利用DESI DR1数据，研究者分析了23个极端发射线星系，通过多元素丰度比揭示了低质量星暴星系中的重子循环过程，发现这些星系处于非平衡、爆发驱动的快速气体循环状态。

papers.title: The fragmentation properties of massive star-forming regions in 30Dor-10 at 2000 au resolution
papers.summary: 该研究首次在银河系外测量核心质量函数，发现其与Salpeter斜率一致，表明恒星质量分布的变异源于演化过程而非初始碎裂

papers.title: Merger rate of initially clustered primordial black holes for the two-body channel
papers.summary: 本文重新评估了原初黑洞（PBH）双星并合率，考虑了PBH聚类的效应，发现在弱聚类情况下可以用更小的PBH丰度解释LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA观测事件，但强聚类会抑制双星通道的并合率。

papers.title: Estimating galactic foreground with the population of resolved galactic binaries
papers.summary: 该研究分析了mHz频段随机引力波背景的探测挑战，特别是银河系内众多致密双星系统产生的混淆前景噪声，提出了基于双星系统空间分布建模前景噪声的方法，并在太极数据挑战II中验证了该方法的可行性。

papers.title: Vaulting the barrier: An intrinsic mechanism to fuel the gas beyond the nuclear ring into the central region of barred galaxies
papers.summary: 通过三维流体动力学模拟发现，棒旋星系中存在气体"跳跃"通道，使部分气体能越过核环直接进入星系中心区域，为核星暴和活动星系核提供燃料。

papers.title: The Quenching Mechanisms of Field Dwarf Galaxies
papers.summary: 在TNG50模拟中发现孤立矮星系存在两种主要淬灭机制：一种是曾被大质量晕捕获的"回溅"星系受环境效应淬灭，另一种是恒星反馈驱动的强气体外流阻止气体冷却形成恒星

papers.title: Gravitational Waves from the Cosmic Dawn: Tracing Cosmic Black Hole Binaries with ET, LGWA and LISA
papers.summary: 研究预测高红移(z≥4)宇宙中黑洞双星并合率与引力波信号，分析黑洞种子、吸积物理和动力学延迟对可探测性的影响，比较爱丁顿极限与超爱丁顿吸积模型下的探测差异。

papers.title: Turning Galaxy Rotation Curves into Radial Cosmic Chronometers: A Nexus Paradigm Approach
papers.summary: 提出了一种将星系旋转曲线转化为径向分辨动力学计时器的方法，可直接从运动学数据重建星系组装历史

papers.title: Tracking ionization balance in intergalactic medium and its implications towards metallicity
papers.summary: 该研究提出了一个快速、包含金属元素的零维框架，用于模拟星系际介质（IGM）的电离平衡和热演化历史，能够准确再现IGM的热和电离历史，并应用于预测宇宙CIV密度参数。

papers.title: Neutrino self-interactions in post-reionization era: Lyman-$α$, 21-cm and cross-spectra
papers.summary: 该研究分析了中微子自相互作用在宇宙早期的影响，通过21厘米强度映射和莱曼α森林观测来约束中微子自相互作用强度，发现CMB+PUMA组合能显著提高约束精度。

papers.title: Age bimodality in pseudo-bulges of barred spiral galaxies: Bar-driven evolution across cosmic time
papers.summary: 研究SDSS DR7中棒旋星系伪核球的恒星族特性，发现棒结构通过向内输送气体促进伪核球形成，随后又通过耗尽或稳定气体流入来抑制中心恒星形成，连接了蓝棒旋星系与红S0星系之间的演化关系。

papers.title: Galactic Rain: Cool Gas Inflows in Red Geyser Galaxies and Their Connection to AGN Activity and Interactions
papers.summary: 红喷泉星系是质量巨大的静止星系，具有大规模但微弱的双对称电离气体外流，被认为是低水平活动星系核反馈的证据。研究发现约70%的冷中性气体正在流入星系中心，流入速度约为47 km/s，且无线电探测的红喷泉星系和受环境影响的星系拥有更大的冷气体储层。

papers.title: 4MOST ChANGES: Catalog of high-redshift quasar candidates (4.5 < $z$ < 7) selected with SED fitting
papers.summary: 该研究通过多波段测光数据和SED拟合，在南方天空识别了6104个高红移类星体候选体（4.5 < z < 7），并通过光谱验证确认了3个z > 5的类星体。

papers.title: Metal enrichment in the galaxy group IC 1262
papers.summary: 利用钱德拉和GMRT观测数据对IC 1262星系群进行金属丰度分析，研究射电喷流、晃动冷锋和激波对金属输运的影响，发现冷锋内外金属丰度差异显著，激波处金属丰度明显下降。

papers.title: Introducing $ΔV_{\star-g}$: a new universal kinematic disturbance parameter
papers.summary: 论文提出新的运动学扰动参数ΔV⋆-g，利用积分视场光谱量化星系恒星与气体速度图的差异，用于探测传统方法可能遗漏的运动学扰动，并应用于MaNGA巡天中的活动星系核分析。

papers.title: Reconstructing chemical enrichment pathways in disc galaxies: A phylogenetic approach
papers.summary: 该研究将生物学中的系统发育方法应用于模拟盘状星系的化学演化分析，通过构建恒星种群的系统发育树来解码星系的组装历史。

papers.title: Impact of Stochastic Pop~III X-ray Binaries on the Cosmological 21-cm Signal
papers.summary: 该研究量化了高红移低恒星形成率区域中高质量X射线双星X射线光度随机性对宇宙黎明21厘米信号的影响，发现随机性增强了小尺度功率谱波动，但对全局信号和大尺度功率谱影响可忽略。

papers.title: Gas-rich ultra-diffuse galaxies: alleviating the MOND tension with HMG
papers.summary: 研究重新评估六个孤立富气超弥散星系在超锥形修正引力理论下的表现，发现HMG模型比MOND表现更好，但仍无法完全解释观测数据。

papers.title: Low-ionization Metal Absorption at $0.7 \lesssim z \lesssim 2$ Confronting Cosmological Simulations with Observations
papers.summary: 研究使用IllustrisTNG宇宙学模拟对比观测数据，评估当前星系形成模型对低电离金属吸收线的再现能力，发现模拟能较好再现中等柱密度范围，但在高等效宽度区域低估了吸收系统的宇宙发生率。
